name: Dermata Therapeutics, Inc.
business: Dermata Therapeutics develops and sells DTC and B2B skincare products under the Tome brand, leveraging its proprietary Bioneedle technology derived from Spongilla lacustris. The company plans to launch a cosmetic Foundational Treatment in mid-2026 and an OTC acne Clearing Treatment shortly thereafter. It also explores a Bioneedle Delivery System for intradermal delivery of macromolecules like botulinum toxin.
sellsTo: medical professionals including aestheticians, med spas, and dermatologists (B2B)

exposures.sentence: In September 2025, after an extensive review of current trends in dermatology, changing consumer preferences, additional non-clinical and clinical development costs, and go-to market costs for an Rx acne product, management, with support from our board of directors, determined that a strategic shift to developing and distributing DTC and B2B skincare products, that are backed by science, would be a better path to commercialization with potentially greater financial upside and faster time to market.

We plan to leverage our Bioneedle platform for broad applicability across dermatologic and aesthetic skin conditions, potentially allowing dermatologists and aestheticians to increase the use of botulinum toxin.

catalysts.event: Launch of first commercial product: Tome Foundational Treatment
catalysts.sentence: We plan to launch our first commercial products, which we expect to be available in the middle of 2026.

catalysts.event: Launch of Tome Clearing Treatment (OTC acne product)
catalysts.sentence: We intend to launch the Clearing Treatment shortly after the launch of our Foundational Treatment.

tamClaims.claim: Global cosmetic and skincare market estimated to reach $736 billion by 2028
tamClaims.sentence: The cosmetic and skincare industry has experienced significant growth in recent years, with global markets estimated to reach $736 billion by 2028.

tamClaims.claim: Acne affects approximately 50 million people in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: Acne is one of the most common dermatologic diseases and can be a significant health burden. Acne is characterized by areas of scaly red skin, non-inflammatory blackheads and whiteheads, inflammatory lesions, papules, and pustules and occasionally cysts and scarring that occur on the face, neck, chest, back, shoulders, and upper arms. It affects approximately 50 million people in the U.S.

tamClaims.claim: U.S. acne treatment products reached approximately $1.7 billion in sales in 2025
tamClaims.sentence: The U.S. acne treatment products reached approximately $1.7 billion in sales in 2025, which was up five percent from the previous year.
